steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The passage opens by establishing what Nixon is most famous for: Watergate.

Then the underlined sentence begins with “But” and declares his legacy is “complex,” followed by evidence of positive achievements.

This “But” marks a direct contrast — the sentence is complicating or challenging the initial perception.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The sentence introduces new information (tribal sovereignty, health care reform) to show that the common public perception of Nixon as primarily defined by Watergate is incomplete.

This is precisely what the correct answer describes: offering information to reveal that a particular perception is an oversimplification.

Inventing an explanation not present in the text, comparing two figures rather than two aspects of one figure, or focusing on Nixon's associates all miss the role of the underlined sentence.

That matches B.
